steps.ordered: Ultrasound Abdomen (LIVER OR GALLBLADDER US (SINGLE ORGAN))
CT Abdomen (CT ABD & PELVIS W & W/O CONTRAST, ADDL SECTIONS)

study_inadequacy.evidence: portions of the pancreatic tail obscured by overlying bowel gas
study_inadequacy.flag: The ultrasound did not fully visualize the pancreas in a patient with lipase 1059 and epigastric pain radiating to the back; the pancreatic tail was not adequately assessed.

alarm_detect.summary: The abdominal ultrasound is technically limited — 'portions of the pancreatic tail obscured by overlying bowel gas' — leaving the pancreas incompletely evaluated despite a lipase of 1059 IU/L, so the imaging question about pancreatic pathology remains open.

alarm_resolve.reason: CT abdomen with contrast directly resolves the study inadequacy by providing cross-sectional imaging of the entire pancreas — including the tail obscured on ultrasound — and the physician's reasoning explicitly targets this gap to characterize pancreatitis severity and exclude underlying structural pathology.
